title: n8n fails to boot after migrating SQLite database
symptom: After migrating the SQLite database to a Docker environment, n8n fails to start properly, often due to permission issues.
applies_when: Migrating n8n from npm to Docker
root_cause: When migrating n8n to Docker, if the permissions of the data directory are not set correctly, n8n may fail to boot. This is a common issue when the ownership of the files does not match the user that the Docker container runs as.
fix_steps: Copy the ~/.n8n/ directory from the old npm server to the new Docker server, ensuring it includes database.sqlite, the config file, and binaryData.
Place the copied directory in /opt/n8n/.n8n on the Docker server.
Update the docker-compose.yml file to mount the directory: - /opt/n8n/.n8n:/home/node/.n8n.
Run the command: chown -R 1000:1000 /opt/n8n/.n8n to fix permissions.
Restart the Docker container with: docker compose down && docker compose up -d.
Check the logs with: docker logs -f n8n-oq2u-n8n-1.
agent_instruction: Ensure the permissions are set correctly and restart the Docker container.
